question.text: Mr. Singh invested Rs. 1,50,000 in an ELSS fund. He is in the 30% tax bracket. What is the maximum tax saving he can achieve under Section 80C from this ELSS investment, including cess?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** ELSS qualifies for deduction under Section 80C up to Rs. 1,50,000 per year. For a 30% tax bracket investor: Tax saving = Rs. 1,50,000 x 30% = Rs. 45,000 + 4% cess = Rs. 45,000 + Rs. 1,800 = Rs. 46,800. ELSS has the shortest lock-in period (3 years) among all Section 80C options.
**The Trap:** Option B forgets cess. Option C uses 20% slab instead of 30%. Option D uses Rs. 2,00,000 limit which is incorrect - Section 80C limit is Rs. 1,50,000.
**Cross-Exam Context:** ELSS has a mandatory 3-year lock-in period, shortest among 80C instruments (PPF: 15 years, NSC: 5 years, Tax-saving FD: 5 years). After lock-in, gains are taxed as equity LTCG.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS offers a triple advantage: tax deduction on investment (Sec 80C), equity market returns potential, and equity LTCG taxation on gains (12.5% with Rs. 1.25 lakh exemption) after the 3-year lock-in.
